DISCOVERY, 610, MOTION ARGUED. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER TO PREVENT THE DEPOSITION OF JERRY KIRBY IS DENIED. THE COURT IS UNABLE TO DETERMINE AT THIS TIME WHETHER THE ONLY RELEVANT TESTIMONY FROM MR. KIRBY CONCERNS THE COMMUNICATIONS DESCRIBED IN THE MARCH 11, 2004 PRIVILEGE LOG THAT HAVE PREVIOUSLY BEEN FOUND BY THE COURT TO BE PROTECTED BY EITHER THE ATTORNEY - CLIENT OR ATTORNEY - WORK PRODUCT PRIVILEGES. PRIOR TO THE COMMENCEMENT OF THE DEPOSITION, THE COURT CAN NEITHER SUSTAIN NOR OVERRULE OBJECTIONS BASED ON AN ASSERTION OF PRIVILEGE. THE REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. COMM: BRUCE E. CHAN
